audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Joseph's self-revelation, divine sending/providence, brotherly reconciliation, Pharaoh's welcome, wagons, and Jacob revived.

verses.restored: Joseph could not restrain himself before all who stood by him, and he cried, Make everyone go out from me. No one stood with him when Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
He gave his voice in weeping, and the Egyptians heard, and Pharaoh’s house heard.
Joseph said to his brothers, I am Joseph. Is my father still alive? But his brothers could not answer him, for they were terrified before his face.
Joseph said to his brothers, Please come near to me. They came near, and he said, I am Joseph your brother, whom you sold into Egypt.
Now do not be grieved or angry in your eyes because you sold me here, for the Divine sent me before you to preserve life.
For these two years the famine has been in the land, and there are still five years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvest.
The Divine sent me before you to set for you a remnant in the earth and to keep you alive by a great escape.
So now, it was not you who sent me here, but the Divine. He has made me a father to Pharaoh, lord of all his house, and ruler over all the land of Egypt.
Hurry and go up to my father and say to him, Thus says your son Joseph: The Divine has made me lord of all Egypt. Come down to me; do not stand still.
You shall dwell in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near me, you and your children, your children’s children, your flocks, your herds, and all that is yours.
There I will sustain you, for there are still five years of famine, lest you and your house and all that is yours become impoverished.
Look, your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin, that it is my mouth speaking to you.
Tell my father of all my weight/glory in Egypt and all that you have seen. Hurry and bring my father down here.
He fell on Benjamin his brother’s neck and wept, and Benjamin wept on his neck.
He kissed all his brothers and wept upon them; afterward his brothers spoke with him.
The sound was heard in Pharaoh’s house: Joseph’s brothers have come. It was good in the eyes of Pharaoh and in the eyes of his servants.
Pharaoh said to Joseph, Say to your brothers, Do this: load your animals and go, enter the land of Canaan.
Take your father and your households and come to me. I will give you the good of the land of Egypt, and you shall eat the fat of the land.
You are commanded: do this. Take wagons from the land of Egypt for your little ones and your women, lift up your father, and come.
Let your eye not pity your goods, for the good of all the land of Egypt is yours.
The sons of Israel did so. Joseph gave them wagons according to Pharaoh’s command and gave them provisions for the way.
To each of them he gave changes of garments, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred silver pieces and five changes of garments.
To his father he sent ten donkeys loaded with the good things of Egypt and ten female donkeys loaded with grain, bread, and food for his father on the way.
He sent his brothers away, and they went. He said to them, Do not be agitated on the way.
They went up from Egypt and came to the land of Canaan, to Jacob their father.
They told him, Joseph is still alive, and he is ruler over all the land of Egypt. His heart went numb, for he did not believe them.
They spoke to him all Joseph’s words that he had spoken to them, and he saw the wagons Joseph had sent to carry him; then the spirit of Jacob their father revived.
Israel said, Enough! Joseph my son is still alive. I will go and see him before I die.
